steps.title: Read the slope of line k
steps.content: The equation $y=-\frac{13}{4}x+6$ is already in slope-intercept form, $y=mx+b$. The slope is the coefficient of $x$, so line $k$ has slope $-\frac{13}{4}$.

steps.title: Use the perpendicular shortcut
steps.content:
Perpendicular slopes are negative reciprocals. Flip $-\frac{13}{4}$ to $-\frac{4}{13}$, then change the sign:
$$m_j=\frac{4}{13}$$

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: The slope of line $j$ is $\frac{4}{13}$, so 4/13 is a correct entry.
